verses.text: Hoshea, the last king of Israel, does evil, though less than some before him. The northern kingdom's final ruler is no worse than the rest — and no better. Its doom is not one man's crime but a whole nation's long unfaithfulness.

Hoshea, the last king of Israel, does evil, though less than some before him. The northern kingdom's final ruler is no worse than the rest — and no better. Its doom is not one man's crime but a whole nation's long unfaithfulness.

Assyria besieges Samaria three years and at last takes it, carrying Israel away to distant lands. The ten tribes go into an exile from which they will not return. Centuries of warning end in the catastrophe the prophets foretold.

Assyria besieges Samaria three years and at last takes it, carrying Israel away to distant lands. The ten tribes go into an exile from which they will not return. Centuries of warning end in the catastrophe the prophets foretold.

Assyria besieges Samaria three years and at last takes it, carrying Israel away to distant lands. The ten tribes go into an exile from which they will not return. Centuries of warning end in the catastrophe the prophets foretold.

The writer pauses to explain: all this came because Israel sinned against the LORD, feared other gods, and walked in the customs of the nations He had cast out. The disaster had a cause. National ruin is the long fruit of forsaking God.

The writer pauses to explain: all this came because Israel sinned against the LORD, feared other gods, and walked in the customs of the nations He had cast out. The disaster had a cause. National ruin is the long fruit of forsaking God.

They did secretly what was not right, built high places everywhere, set up images, and served idols, though the LORD had said, 'Ye shall not do this thing.' They sinned against clear light. Persistent disobedience to a known command invites certain judgment.

They did secretly what was not right, built high places everywhere, set up images, and served idols, though the LORD had said, 'Ye shall not do this thing.' They sinned against clear light. Persistent disobedience to a known command invites certain judgment.

They did secretly what was not right, built high places everywhere, set up images, and served idols, though the LORD had said, 'Ye shall not do this thing.' They sinned against clear light. Persistent disobedience to a known command invites certain judgment.

Yet the LORD had testified by every prophet, 'Turn from your evil ways' — and they would not hear, but stiffened their necks like their fathers. God warned long and patiently before He struck. The fall came only after generations of refused mercy.

Yet the LORD had testified by every prophet, 'Turn from your evil ways' — and they would not hear, but stiffened their necks like their fathers. God warned long and patiently before He struck. The fall came only after generations of refused mercy.

Yet the LORD had testified by every prophet, 'Turn from your evil ways' — and they would not hear, but stiffened their necks like their fathers. God warned long and patiently before He struck. The fall came only after generations of refused mercy.

They forsook all His commandments, made calves and an idol-grove, worshiped the host of heaven and Baal, burned their children, and used divination, selling themselves to do evil. The catalogue is staggering. A people can sink to depths that make their judgment plainly just.

They forsook all His commandments, made calves and an idol-grove, worshiped the host of heaven and Baal, burned their children, and used divination, selling themselves to do evil. The catalogue is staggering. A people can sink to depths that make their judgment plainly just.

So the LORD removed Israel from His sight, leaving only Judah — yet Judah too kept Israel's sins, and He would afflict them as well. The northern fall was a warning the south refused to learn. To witness another's judgment and not repent is to invite the same.

So the LORD removed Israel from His sight, leaving only Judah — yet Judah too kept Israel's sins, and He would afflict them as well. The northern fall was a warning the south refused to learn. To witness another's judgment and not repent is to invite the same.

So the LORD removed Israel from His sight, leaving only Judah — yet Judah too kept Israel's sins, and He would afflict them as well. The northern fall was a warning the south refused to learn. To witness another's judgment and not repent is to invite the same.

From the day Jeroboam tore Israel from David's house and made them sin with his calves, they never departed from it, till the LORD removed them as the prophets warned. One king's founding sin shaped a nation's doom. The course set at the start ran on to its bitter end.

Assyria resettles foreigners in Samaria; lions attack them, so a captured priest is sent to teach 'the manner of the God of the land.' They want religion as insurance, not devotion. Many seek to appease God without truly knowing or loving Him.

Yet every nation makes its own gods and sets them in the high places — a clutter of idols beside the LORD's name. They add the true God to their collection. To worship Him alongside other gods is not worship but insult.

Yet every nation makes its own gods and sets them in the high places — a clutter of idols beside the LORD's name. They add the true God to their collection. To worship Him alongside other gods is not worship but insult.

Yet every nation makes its own gods and sets them in the high places — a clutter of idols beside the LORD's name. They add the true God to their collection. To worship Him alongside other gods is not worship but insult.

So 'they feared the LORD, and served their own gods' — a divided heart that pleased neither. They kept both, committed to neither. God will not share His glory; a faith mixed with idols is no faith at all.

So 'they feared the LORD, and served their own gods' — a divided heart that pleased neither. They kept both, committed to neither. God will not share His glory; a faith mixed with idols is no faith at all.

So 'they feared the LORD, and served their own gods' — a divided heart that pleased neither. They kept both, committed to neither. God will not share His glory; a faith mixed with idols is no faith at all.

The LORD's command had been plain: fear Him only, who brought you out of Egypt with great power, and bow to no other gods, and He will deliver you. The covenant asked single-hearted loyalty. All that follows shows the cost of refusing it.

The LORD's command had been plain: fear Him only, who brought you out of Egypt with great power, and bow to no other gods, and He will deliver you. The covenant asked single-hearted loyalty. All that follows shows the cost of refusing it.

But they would not hear; to this day they fear the LORD and serve their graven images, as their fathers did. The divided heart is handed down. A half-religion, neither hot nor cold, becomes the inheritance of generations until someone breaks it.

But they would not hear; to this day they fear the LORD and serve their graven images, as their fathers did. The divided heart is handed down. A half-religion, neither hot nor cold, becomes the inheritance of generations until someone breaks it.
